title: Printing machine assistants
description: Research, study, and prepare maps and other spatial data in digital or graphic form for one or more purposes, such as legal, social, political, educational, and design purposes. May work with Geographic Information Systems (GIS). May design and evaluate algorithms, data structures, and user interfaces for GIS and mapping systems. May collect, analyze, and interpret geographic information provided by geodetic surveys, aerial photographs, and satellite data. Make exact measurements and determine property boundaries. Provide data relevant to the shape, contour, gravitation, location, elevation, or dimension of land or land features on or near the earth's surface for engineering, mapmaking, mining, land evaluation, construction, and other purposes. Measure large areas of the Earth's surface using satellite observations, global navigation satellite systems (GNSS), light detection and ranging (LIDAR), or related sources. (Based on 24 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct surveys to determine exact positions, including measurement of points, elevations, lines, areas, volumes, contours, or other features of land surfaces, and determine orientation of tracts of land using surveying instruments.

Analyze survey data to ensure adherence to project specifications or land survey standards, and assess the quality of control data to determine the need for additional survey data for engineering, construction, or other projects.

Calculate the exact horizontal and vertical position of points on the Earth's surface and compute geodetic measurements to interpret survey data for positions, shapes, and elevations of underlying land features.
Maintain databases of geodetic and related information and clean and maintain equipment to ensure operational efficiency and data integrity.
Train assistants and helpers in executing surveys or drafting maps, and supervise preparation of all data and documents related to surveys.
Revise existing maps and charts, making all necessary corrections and adjustments, and delineate aerial photographic detail using precision drafting instruments.
Prepare and alter trace maps, charts, detailed drawings, and three-dimensional models of terrain using stereoscopic plotting and computer graphics equipment.
Collect information about specific features of the Earth through methods such as aerial photography and remote sensing, and carry out research in surveying and mapping methods.
Review computer-processed digital images for quality and specify number of images to be printed and direct to printer using computer software.
Inspect final compositions and examine completed work to detect defects and verify conformance to work orders, making adjustments to machinery as necessary for quality assurance.
Load, unload, or adjust materials or products on conveyors and maintain production and inspection records for operations.
Establish fixed points for use in making maps, using geodetic and engineering instruments, and monitor equipment operations to ensure precision in measurement.
Direct aerial surveys of specified geographical areas and determine specifications for equipment to be used for aerial photography.
Identify, scale, and orient geodetic points, elevations, and other planimetric or topographic features applying mathematical formulas applied in surveying.
Conduct quality control checks on equipment to detect malfunctions, providing solutions to restore operational status and ensure accuracy in measurement instruments.
Prepare progress or technical reports related to surveying and geospatial data analysis for projects, ensuring records of surveys are maintained for legal considerations.
Distribute geodetic data to relevant government agencies or stakeholders, maintaining effective communication regarding project advancements.
Examine and analyze data from ground surveys, reports, and other data to prepare topographic maps and related documents, documenting specific land features accurately.
Plan and conduct ground surveys designed to establish baselines, elevations, and other geodetic measurements to support engineering or land evaluation projects.
Mentor and provide technical consultation to surveying staff while directing project workflows, ensuring adherence to established standards.
Testify as an expert witness in court cases on land survey issues, providing authoritative information on property boundaries and surveying standards.

Create prints according to customer specifications and laboratory protocols, and generate prepress proofs in digital or other format to approximate the appearance of the final printed piece. Maintain records of materials used and processing completed, and examine developed prints for defects, such as broken lines, spots, or blurs. Proofread and perform quality control of text and images, and perform a "preflight" check of required font, graphic, text, and image files to ensure completeness prior to delivery to printer. Monitor and record temperatures, amounts of materials processed, and test results; maintain, adjust, and clean printing equipment, and perform minor repairs as needed.

Load circuit boards, racks or rolls of film, negatives, or printing paper into processing or printing machines, and load digital images onto computers directly from cameras or from storage devices. Start machines, and turn valves or move controls to feed, admit, apply, or transfer materials and adhesives. Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and report or resolve problems, and observe gauges, meters, and control panels to obtain information about equipment statuses.

Weigh or measure materials and products using scales or other measuring instruments, or visually estimate their weight. Grade and sort products according to factors such as color, species, length, and quality to ensure correct processing and usage. Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, and remove jammed materials from machines and readjust components as necessary to resume operations.

Select, mix, and apply coatings such as inks or adhesives to protect or decorate surfaces of workpieces using spray guns, pens, or brushes. Adjust machine components according to specifications for widths, lengths, thicknesses of materials, and amounts of glue or adhesive required. Clean ink fountains, plates, or printing unit cylinders when press runs are completed, and examine job orders to determine quantities to be printed and special printing instructions.

Start presses and pull proofs to check for ink coverage and density, alignment, and registration. Collect and inspect random samples during print runs to identify any necessary adjustments and secure printing plates to printing units while adjusting tolerances as required. Monitor and verify that paper and ink meet specifications set for each job, and adjust controls to maintain specified conditions throughout the printing process.

Transport materials, supplies, and finished products between storage and work areas using forklifts. Examine finished surfaces of workpieces to verify conformity to specifications and retouch any defective areas. Maintain production records such as quantities, dimensions, and thicknesses of materials processed.

Collaborate with graphic artists, editors, and writers to produce master copies according to design specifications, and edit graphics and photos using pixel or bitmap editing, airbrushing, masking, or image retouching. Import text and art elements from various databases in a visually appealing way, and position, enter, and alter text size using computer systems to arrange pages correctly for printing.

Monitor inventory levels regularly and order additional supplies as necessary. Clean and maintain tools and equipment using solvents, brushes, and rags to ensure they remain in good working condition.

Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, drill presses, or CNC machines, to fabricate parts or prototypes according to specifications, ensuring that parts meet precise dimensions and finishes by conducting test runs and making necessary adjustments.

Visualize, measure, mark, and scribe dimensions on materials such as metal or plastic to lay out machining, using instruments like protractors, micrometers, and rulers, while computing layout dimensions and determining reference points for further processing.

File, grind, shim, and adjust parts to fit them together properly, inspecting finished work for defects and smoothness, and performing preventative maintenance or minor repairs on machines as necessary.

Design jigs, fixtures, and templates as work aids for the fabrication of parts or products, and prepare geometric layouts from graphic displays or blueprints using computer-aided drafting software or hand drafting instruments.

Source and select materials based on their physical properties for use in fabrication, and cut, shape, or trim these materials to specified lengths or shapes to prepare them for further processing into final products.

Inspect and analyze the quality of fabricated parts to ensure conformance to specifications, marking any defects or non-conformities, and preparing reports or records of findings for quality assurance.

Collaborate with other team members to modify existing processes or setups to enhance production efficiency and reduce material waste, while also managing the setup of tooling and machine order for jobs as necessary.

Use computer programs to effectively manage the coding for machine operations, including writing and revising programs for cuts, drilling, and other operations, while ensuring compatibility with machine specifications and capabilities.

Examine designs, materials, and customer specifications in the production process, ensuring that all parts are aligned and assembled properly using cutting tools and ensuring that all requirements are met for the finished product.

emerging_tasks.task: Determine orientation of tracts of land, including position, boundaries, size, and shape, using theodolites, electronic distance-measuring equipment, satellite-based positioning equipment, drones, land information systems, or other geodetic survey equipment.
Estimate resources, such as production hours, required for projects.
Use drone technology to capture high-resolution images and data for map creation and updating.
Correct color in photographs or digital images.
Read blueprints and project specification manuals to recommend appropriate hardware and electronic locking devices.
